title: MG_HW10: Annotate anvio gene calls for function and taxonomy
steps: Login to the HPC ssh hpcice ssh hpc ice
Move into your user directory in bh_class cd /rsgrps/bh_class/user_id

We are going to run analyses on the anvio gene calls to get function and taxonomy. Create these directories to store the analyses. mkdir taxonomy-genesmkdir taxonomy-genes/std-errmkdir taxonomy-genes/std-outmkdir anvio-genesmkdir function-genesmkdir function-genes/std-errmkdir function-genes/std-out mkdir taxonomy-genes mkdir taxonomy-genes/std-err mkdir taxonomy-genes/std-out mkdir anvio-genes mkdir function-genes mkdir function-genes/std-err mkdir function-genes/std-out

First we will need to download the anvio gene calls. Open Anvio on your computer. And type the following commands in the anvio terminal window. anvi-get-dna-sequences-for-gene-calls -c contigs.db -o nucleotides.faa
How many genes did Anvi'o find on your contigs? Check by entering the following command in the Anvi'o terminal. egrep
From your anvio terminal, you need to secure copy the anvio-genes.faa file back to the HPC. scp nucleotides.faa sftp.hpc.arizona.edu:/rsgrps/bh_class/username/anvio-genes
Go to the HPC and make sure your anvio-genes.faa file is there. cd /rsgrps/bh_class/username/anvio-genesls -l nucleotides.faa cd /rsgrps/bh_class/username/anvio-genes ls -l nucleotides.faa
Go to the taxonomy-genes directory to perform the taxonomic analysis on the genes using centrifuge. cd /rsgrps/bh_class/bhurwitz/taxonomy-genes

Create the following script to run taxonomic analysis on the gene calls from Anvi'o. #!/bin/bash#PBS -W group_list=bh_class#PBS -q windfall#PBS -l jobtype=cluster_only#PBS -l select=1:ncpus=12:mem=23gb#PBS -l pvmem=22gb#PBS -l walltime=24:00:00#PBS -l cput=24:00:00#PBS -M netid@email.arizona.edu#PBS -m bea#--------------EDIT THESE---------------FASTA= #!/bin/bash #PBS -W group_list=bh_class #PBS -q windfall #PBS -l jobtype=cluster_only #PBS -l select=1:ncpus=12:mem=23gb #PBS -l pvmem=22gb #PBS -l walltime=24:00:00 #PBS -l cput=24:00:00 #PBS -M netid@email.arizona.edu #PBS -m bea #--------------EDIT THESE--------------- FASTA="/rsgrps/bh_class/username/anvio-genes/nucleotides.faa" OUT_DIR="/rsgrps/bh_class/username/taxonomy-genes" #--------------------------------------- CENT_DB="/rsgrps/bh_class/b_compressed+h+v/b_compressed+h+v" centrifuge -x $CENT_DB -U $FASTA -S $OUT_DIR/centrifuge_hits.tsv --report-file $OUT_DIR/centrifuge_report.tsv -f

Run the centrifuge_tax.sh script to get the taxonomic information for genes. chmod 755 centrifuge_tax.shqsub -o std-out/ -e std-err/ centrifuge_tax.sh chmod 755 centrifuge_tax.sh qsub -o std-out/ -e std-err/ centrifuge_tax.sh
Go to the function-genes directory. We are going to get the top pfam and kegg ids for each contig using the program uproc. cd /rsgrps/bh_class/username/function-genes

Create uproc_function.sh to run the functional analysis. #!/bin/bash#PBS -W group_list=bh_class#PBS -q windfall#PBS -l jobtype=cluster_only#PBS -l select=1:ncpus=12:mem=23gb#PBS -l pvmem=22gb#PBS -l walltime=24:00:00#PBS -l cput=24:00:00#PBS -M netid@email.arizona.edu#PBS -m bea#--------------EDIT THESE---------------FASTA= #!/bin/bash #PBS -W group_list=bh_class #PBS -q windfall #PBS -l jobtype=cluster_only #PBS -l select=1:ncpus=12:mem=23gb #PBS -l pvmem=22gb #PBS -l walltime=24:00:00 #PBS -l cput=24:00:00 #PBS -M netid@email.arizona.edu #PBS -m bea #--------------EDIT THESE--------------- FASTA="/rsgrps/bh_class/username/anvio-genes/nucleotides.faa" OUT_DIR="/rsgrps/bh_class/username/function-genes" OUTPUT="$OUT_DIR/uproc-out" #--------------------------------------- export UPROC="/rsgrps/bh_class/bin/uproc-dna" export DATA="/rsgrps/bh_class/data/uproc" export UPROC_MODEL="$DATA/model" export UPROC_OUT_DIR="$OUT_DIR/uproc-out" export PFAM="$DATA/pfam27ready" export KEGG="$DATA/keggready" $UPROC --preds --short --threads 12 --output $OUTPUT.pfam $PFAM $UPROC_MODEL $FASTA $UPROC --preds --short --threads 12 --output $OUTPUT.kegg $KEGG $UPROC_MODEL $FASTA
Run the uproc_function.sh script chmod 755 uproc_function.shqsub -o std-out/ -e std-err/ uproc_function.sh chmod 755 uproc_function.sh qsub -o std-out/ -e std-err/ uproc_function.sh

Create a perl script called format-anvio.pl to convert the functional data into anvio format. #! /uaopt/perl/5.14.2/bin/perluse strict;if (@ARGV != 4) { die $out") || die "Cannot open out
"; my %id_to_desc; while () { chomp $_; my ($id, $desc) = split (/	/, $_); $id_to_desc{$id} = $desc; } print OUT "gene_callers_id	source	accession	function	e_value
"; while () { chomp $_; my @fields = split (/,/, $_); my $gene = $fields[1]; $gene =~ s/\|.*//; my $id = $fields[6]; my $score = $fields[7]; my $desc = "NONE"; if (exists $id_to_desc{$id}) { $desc = $id_to_desc{$id}; } print OUT "$gene	$source	$id	$desc	$score
"; }" description="" os_version="" os_name="" guid="CF20F5C29762404A91449958868D3740" > #! /uaopt/perl/5.14.2/bin/perl use strict; if (@ARGV != 4) { die "Usage: format-anvio.pl uproc-file function-to-desc out source
"; } my $uproc = shift @ARGV; my $function = shift @ARGV; my $out = shift @ARGV; my $source = shift @ARGV; open (F, $function) || die "I need a kegg or pfam desc file
"; open (U, $uproc) || die "I need the uproc file
"; open (OUT, ">$out") || die "Cannot open out
"; my %id_to_desc; while () { chomp $_; my ($id, $desc) = split (/	/, $_); $id_to_desc{$id} = $desc; } print OUT "gene_callers_id	source	accession	function	e_value
"; while () { chomp $_; my @fields = split (/,/, $_); my $gene = $fields[1]; $gene =~ s/\|.*//; my $id = $fields[6]; my $score = $fields[7]; my $desc = "NONE"; if (exists $id_to_desc{$id}) { $desc = $id_to_desc{$id}; } print OUT "$gene	$source	$id	$desc	$score
"; }

Run format-anvio.pl to convert the functional data into anvio format. chmod 755 format-anvio.pl./format-anvio.pl uproc-out.kegg /rsgrps/bh_class/kegg_to_desc uproc-kegg-anvio kegg./format-anvio.pl uproc-out.pfam /rsgrps/bh_class/pfam_to_domain uproc-pfam-anvio pfamcat uproc-kegg-anvio > input_matrix.txtegrep -v > input_matrix.txt" description="" os_version="" os_name="" guid="DBF8B67E1C4643659C0793F8C0F511C2" > chmod 755 format-anvio.pl ./format-anvio.pl uproc-out.kegg /rsgrps/bh_class/kegg_to_desc uproc-kegg-anvio kegg ./format-anvio.pl uproc-out.pfam /rsgrps/bh_class/pfam_to_domain uproc-pfam-anvio pfam cat uproc-kegg-anvio > input_matrix.txt egrep -v "gene_callers_id" uproc-pfam-anvio >> input_matrix.txt

Download the functional data to your computer. Go to the directory where Anvi'o is running (on your computer) and type the following commands to download the files. scp netid@sftp.hpc.arizona.edu:/rsgrps/bh_class/username/function-genes/input_matrix.txt . Note the I use a

Download the taxonomic data to your computer. Go to the directory where Anvi'o is running (on your computer) and type the following commands to download the files. scp netid@sftp.hpc.arizona.edu:/rsgrps/bh_class/username/taxonomy-genes/centrifuge_report.tsv .scp netid@sftp.hpc.arizona.edu:/rsgrps/bh_class/username/taxonomy-genes/centrifuge_hits.tsv . Note the I use a scp netid@sftp.hpc.arizona.edu:/rsgrps/bh_class/username/taxonomy-genes/centrifuge_report.tsv . scp netid@sftp.hpc.arizona.edu:/rsgrps/bh_class/username/taxonomy-genes/centrifuge_hits.tsv . Note the I use a "." here to mean the current directory in Anvi'o, on my computer this is: /Users/bhurwitz/skinmicrobiome
From the Anvi'o terminal, type the following command to upload the taxonomic data for the gene calls. anvi-import-taxonomy -c contigs.db -i centrifuge_report.tsv centrifuge_hits.tsv -p centrifuge
From the Anvi'o terminal, type the following command to upload the functional data for the gene calls. anvi-import-functions -c contigs.db -i input_matrix.txt

Please document these steps in the methods section of your report. Note what programs you used for each step and what the parameters were. How many genes did Anvi'o find? Is this different from your analyses? Why? Anvi'o uses the "-p meta" parameter for metagenomics datasets, which is stricter than what you first ran. How many of your genes from Anvio had a match to a known bacteria? How many matched known proteins for kegg or pfam?
creator: Bonnie L Hurwitz
